channel.title: Posts on Shit PH Said
channel.description: Recent content in Posts on Shit PH Said

item.title: Cabinet Maintains RON95 at RM1.99 — Anwar Calls It a 'Difficult Decision' While Dodging Subsidy Reform for the Third Year Running
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister and Finance Minister

Category: Economy

Date: 2026-03-11

📌 What Happened

Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim announced on March 11, 2026 — following a Cabinet meeting — that the price of RON95 petrol under the Budi95 targeted subsidy mechanism will remain at RM1.99 per litre. He called it a “difficult decision,” noting that the market price of RON95 has risen roughly threefold due to the escalating US-Iran conflict disrupting oil markets and threatening Gulf shipping routes. RON97 had already risen to RM3.25, unsubsidised RON95 to RM2.67, and diesel to RM3.12. Anwar framed the decision as a sacrifice for the rakyat.

item.title: Oil Past $100, Strait of Hormuz Shut, Rakyat Burning Fuel to Commute — Anwar Won't Mandate WFH, Freeze Tolls, Cap Food Prices, or Cut GLC Fat
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister and Finance Minister

Category: Economy

Date: 2026-03-11

📌 What Happened

The Iran-US war has pushed global oil past $100 per barrel, effectively shut down the Strait of Hormuz — through which nearly 80% of Asia’s oil transits — and sent Malaysia’s FBM KLCI plunging 2.55% in a single session on March 9. RON97 is up to RM3.25, diesel to RM3.12, and Anwar himself admitted on March 7 that the government can only hold RON95 at RM1.99 for ‘one or two more months.’ Freight costs are surging, food prices are next, and experts from NST to Hong Leong IB are warning of imported inflation hitting the rakyat’s kitchen table. Anwar’s response so far: a strongly worded tweet about solidarity, an emergency parliamentary motion condemning Israel, and Zafrul saying Malaysia is ‘prepared’ — without naming a single concrete domestic measure to protect workers and families from the economic fallout.

item.title: PH Backbenchers Call for Snap Election Dressed Up as 'Reform' — Hold GE16 With All State Polls, Save Money, They Say
item.description:
Said by: Azli Yusof (Amanah-Shah Alam) / Jimmy Puah (PKR-Tebrau)

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-03-11

📌 What Happened

On March 11, two Pakatan Harapan backbenchers — Shah Alam MP Azli Yusof (Amanah) and Tebrau MP Jimmy Puah (PKR) — called for all state assembly elections to be held concurrently with the 16th General Election. Speaking to FMT, both MPs argued that separate elections lead to lower voter turnout, waste public money, and produce state governments with weaker mandates. Azli said separate polls are “unfair to voters, especially those who live far away from where they vote.” Puah called it “simply too tiring for people to come out and vote twice.” Putrajaya spent RM725 million on GE15 in 2022 and a further RM420 million on six subsequent state elections in 2023.

item.title: Police Question C4 Anti-Corruption CEO Under Section 124B — Anwar's Government Uses 'Plot to Topple' Probe to Interrogate Civil Society
item.description:
Said by: Bukit Aman (Royal Malaysia Police) / C4 Center (Pushpan Murugiah, CEO)

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-03-11

📌 What Happened

On March 10, 2026, the Center to Combat Corruption and Cronyism (C4) CEO Pushpan Murugiah was summoned to Bukit Aman and questioned for approximately 90 minutes under Section 124B of the Penal Code — activities detrimental to parliamentary democracy. The questioning was linked to the police investigation into an alleged plot to topple the government, reportedly because Pushpan’s name appeared in an email deemed relevant to the probe. But the interrogation went far beyond that email. Police questioned C4’s sources of funding, whether the organisation had received instructions to issue statements, and — remarkably — challenged C4’s right to comment on matters of national governance. C4 confirmed that several other political commentators and academics had also been summoned to Bukit Aman under the same investigation.

item.title: Sabah Rejected PH at the Ballot Box — Now Mustapha Sakmud Says He'll Be Their Voice in Putrajaya Anyway
item.description:
Said by: Mustapha Sakmud, Sabah PH Chairman / Sabah and Sarawak Affairs Minister

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-03-11

📌 What Happened

On March 11, 2026, Sabah PH chairman and Sabah and Sarawak Affairs Minister Mustapha Sakmud told FMT that Sabah PH will serve as a “conduit” to ensure state issues are voiced at the federal level. He said he intends to leverage his ministerial appointment — made on February 15 — to bring Sabahan concerns to Cabinet meetings and government agencies. “This is the role we aim to play, and we hope the people of Sabah will appreciate it when their fundamental needs are addressed,” he said.

item.title: 99-Year-Old Kampung Lee Kong Chian Residents Told to Get Out — RM50K Per Family, Hannah Yeoh 'Unable to Attend'
item.description:
Said by: Lo Su Fui, Deputy Minister (Federal Territories) / Hannah Yeoh, Minister (Federal Territories)

Category: Housing

Date: 2026-03-10

📌 What Happened

More than 150 residents of Kampung Lee Kong Chian in Setapak — Kuala Lumpur’s sole surviving private Chinese village, established in 1927 as quarters for Lee Rubber Group factory workers — held a peaceful rally on 8 March 2026 after 146 households received eviction notices in November 2025 ordering them to vacate by September. The land has been sold to a private company. Residents were offered RM50,000 per household in compensation, which they rejected, demanding People’s Housing Project (PPR) units instead. Federal Territories Minister Hannah Yeoh was absent from the village’s final Chap Goh Meh celebration on 10 March; Deputy Minister Lo Su Fui showed up, handed over RM10,000 for the dinner, and said he would “bring this matter back to the Federal Territories Department to explore how we can best provide assistance.” The government’s official position, per NST, is that it will “review the situation before deciding whether to intervene.”

item.title: DAP Finally Demands RCI on MACC — Weeks Late, and PAS Correctly Calls It Political Theatre
item.description:
Said by: DAP Leadership / Ahmad Fadhli Shaari, PAS Information Chief

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-03-10

📌 What Happened

On March 10, DAP — the largest party in the Pakatan Harapan coalition with 40 parliamentary seats — formally called for a Royal Commission of Inquiry (RCI) to investigate the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission over allegations first reported by Bloomberg, including that MACC chief Azam Baki breached civil servant shareholding limits and that the agency was involved in corporate intimidation. PAS information chief Ahmad Fadhli Shaari responded by calling the demand “political theatre,” noting that DAP had remained silent when the same Bloomberg reports alleged that Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim personally interfered in MACC investigations.

item.title: Dzulkefly Promises — Again — to Crack Down on Workplace Bullying in Government Hospitals
item.description:
Said by: Dzulkefly Ahmad, Health Minister

Category: Health

Date: 2026-03-10

📌 What Happened

Health Minister Dzulkefly Ahmad announced on March 10, 2026 that the Health Ministry will “bolster enforcement” against workplace bullying in government health facilities. He said public hospitals should be “professional and ethical workplace environments” and pointed to a Public Services Department (PSD) circular that bans coarse language and verbal abuse in the workplace. He pledged to “enhance implementation” of the circular to ensure “no ambiguity” in handling misconduct — language that strongly implies the existing rules are not being enforced.

item.title: Rafizi Quietly Drops NFCorp Bankruptcy Bid — The Anti-Corruption Crusader Makes Nice With the People He Once Exposed
item.description:
Said by: Rafizi Ramli, Pandan MP / Former Economy Minister

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-03-10

📌 What Happened

Rafizi Ramli, the Pandan MP and former Economy Minister who built his political career on exposing the National Feedlot Corporation (NFCorp) scandal, has quietly dropped his bankruptcy and winding-up proceedings against NFCorp director Datuk Seri Dr Mohamad Salleh Ismail and the company itself. A Notice of Discontinuance was filed on March 3, and the High Court struck out the case on March 10 without an order as to costs. Rafizi’s lawyer Razlan Hadri Zulkifli confirmed the matter was “amicably resolved.” The case stemmed from court-ordered costs owed to Rafizi after the Federal Court upheld his appeal in April 2022 — NFCorp and Salleh had paid RM300,000 in December 2022 but stopped paying the outstanding interest balance.

item.title: Ramanan Claims 'Paid Agents' Are Behind Racial Tensions — Promises Cabinet Crackdown Instead of Addressing Root Causes
item.description:
Said by: R. Ramanan, Human Resources Minister / Aaron Ago Dagang, National Unity Minister

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-03-10

📌 What Happened

Human Resources Minister and PKR Vice-President Datuk Seri R. Ramanan held a special press conference in Putrajaya on March 9 flanked by PKR officials, claiming that “agents of provocation” are being paid to incite racial and religious tensions in Malaysia. He said the matter would be raised at Cabinet on March 11, and that arrests have already been made. National Unity Minister Aaron Ago Dagang echoed the call on March 10, urging police to “take stern action” against anyone disrupting harmony. Neither minister named the alleged provocateurs or their financiers — Ramanan specifically said he would not name them to avoid “making them famous.”

item.title: Anwar Announces RM500 Cash Handout for Civil Servants and Mosque Staff — Hari Raya Election Season Begins
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister and Finance Minister

Category: Economy

Date: 2026-03-09

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: On 9 March 2026, Anwar Ibrahim announced Special Financial Assistance (Bantuan Kewangan Khas, BKK) 2026: RM500 for civil servants Grade 15 and below, RM250 for pensioners and veterans, and RM500 for approximately 70,000 mosque and religious personnel — imams, bilal, siak, marbut, KAFA and takmir teachers. Payments land on 13 March, days before Hari Raya Aidilfitri, when household spending peaks and political goodwill is most visible. Anwar holds both the PM and Finance Minister portfolios, giving him unilateral authority over the timing and targeting of such disbursements.

item.title: Anwar Brings Forward RM15 Billion STR Cash Handout — Record Spending, Hari Raya Timing, Snap Election Vibes
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister and Finance Minister

Category: Economy

Date: 2026-03-09

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: On March 9, 2026, Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim announced that Phase 2 of the Sumbangan Tunai Rahmah (STR) cash transfer programme would be brought forward to March 10 — weeks ahead of its originally scheduled date. The early disbursement will benefit 5.2 million recipients.

Anwar framed the move as helping Malaysians prepare for Hari Raya Aidilfitri. The total 2026 allocation for STR and SARA (Sumbangan Asas Rahmah) stands at RM15 billion — the highest in the history of the Federal Government’s direct cash assistance programmes.

item.title: Anwar Condemns Iran's Attacks on Kuwait — Weeks After Parliament Motion Defending Iran Against US-Israel Strikes
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-03-09

📌 What Happened

On March 9, 2026, Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim called the Prime Minister of Kuwait, Sheikh Ahmad Abdullah Al-Ahmad Al-Sabah, to condemn Iranian attacks targeting Kuwait. He expressed condolences for the casualties and affirmed Malaysia’s support for Kuwait’s security and territorial integrity. This phone call came exactly one week after Anwar tabled an emergency motion in Parliament on March 2 condemning US-Israel strikes on Iran, during which he declared that Malaysia could ’no longer swallow’ Western human rights lectures and PH backbencher Syahredzan Johan called on MPs to boycott all US embassy events.

item.title: Anwar Launches Capital Market Masterplan, Says 'Reform Non-Negotiable' — While Every Actual Reform Gets Delayed or Killed
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister

Category: Economy

Date: 2026-03-09

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim delivered the keynote address at the launch of the Securities Commission’s Capital Market Masterplan 2026–2030 (CMP4) on March 9, 2026. He declared that “the government has made reform non-negotiable” and that Malaysia’s capital market “must drive shared prosperity, not just profit.”

The CMP4 targets growing Malaysia’s capital market to RM6.3 trillion by 2030 and includes initiatives like the “MY Value Up” program to boost PLC valuations.

item.title: Anwar Repeats 'Aid Based on Need, Not Race' in Dewan Negara — Same Script, Different Session, Zero Structural Change
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-03-09

📌 WHAT HAPPENED

Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim told the Dewan Negara on March 9, 2026, that government aid and assistance are allocated based on need, not race. He affirmed that the Madani government would “remain fair to all states” and that assistance targets those most in need regardless of background.

This is not the first time Anwar has used this exact line. In August 2025, he told the same Dewan Negara the same thing — almost word for word — as reported by The Star and NST. Before that, the 13th Malaysia Plan (13MP) was framed around the same “needs-based, not race-based” rhetoric.

item.title: Anwar Won't Suspend Azam Baki — Will Just Let His Contract Expire in May While the Country Burns
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister / Straits Times sources

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-03-09

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: The Straits Times reported on March 9, 2026 — citing high-level government and legal sources — that Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim has decided not to renew MACC chief commissioner Tan Sri Azam Baki’s tenure when it expires on May 12, 2026. This would mark the end of Azam’s fourth annual extension since reaching retirement age in 2023. According to the report, Anwar has “repeatedly stressed to Cabinet in recent weeks that Azam won’t be renewed.” But crucially, Anwar is not suspending Azam now. He is not ordering an independent public inquiry. He is not referring the matter to Parliament. He is simply going to let the clock run out — two more months of an anti-corruption chief accused of being the head of a “corporate mafia.” This comes after Bloomberg reported that the MACC under Azam had abused its powers to extort and manipulate corporate figures; Azam was found to have breached civil servant shareholding rules multiple times, most recently in January 2026, holding 17.7 million shares in Velocity Capital Bhd; former Economy Minister Rafizi Ramli and 8 PKR MPs publicly demanded Azam’s immediate suspension; Bersih demanded resignation and organised protests; DAP Secretary-General Anthony Loke called the government’s response “inadequate”; and a Feb 15 rally in Kuala Lumpur demanded Azam’s arrest.

item.title: Anwar's Government Fights 531,976 Retirees in Court — Wins Appeal to Block RM1.7 Billion Pension Payout
item.description:
Said by: Federal Government / Senior Federal Counsel Sallehuddin Ali

Category: Economy

Date: 2026-03-09

📌 What Happened

On 9 March 2026, the Court of Appeal unanimously overturned a High Court order that would have required Anwar Ibrahim’s government to pay RM1.7 billion in pension arrears to 531,976 former civil servants. The three-judge panel — led by Justice Azhahari Kamal Ramli, with Justices Lim Chong Fong and Meor Hashimi Abdul Hamid — ruled that the pensioners’ application was res judicata (already adjudicated) and constituted an “abuse of court process.” The government, represented by Senior Federal Counsel Sallehuddin Ali, successfully argued that the 2016 Public Service Department Circular (PP 1/2016) was an “enhancement” to the civil service scheme, not a mandatory salary revision triggering pension adjustments. The original case was brought by Aminah Ahmad, a retired Ministry of Foreign Affairs officer, on behalf of 56 other retirees in 2017.

item.title: Daim's Family Sues Anwar's Government Over 'Zionist Plot' Claims — Calls Police Probe Unlawful and Unconstitutional
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister / Daim Zainuddin’s Family (Respondents)

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-03-09

📌 What Happened

The family of late former finance minister Daim Zainuddin — his widow Naimah Abdul Khalid, two sons, and two family lawyers — filed a lawsuit at the Kuala Lumpur High Court on March 7, 2026, seeking a declaration that the police investigation into the alleged plot to topple Anwar’s government is unlawful, unconstitutional, and tainted by bad faith. This comes after Anwar told Parliament on March 3 that a suspect in the alleged conspiracy had engaged an international PR firm to launch coordinated attacks on national institutions, claiming the plot involved ‘a prominent Zionist group,’ foreign lobbyists, bankers, and international media agencies.

item.title: DAP Brings Forward Congress to July — Will Vote on Whether Leaders Should Quit Government, But Promises to Stay Anyway
item.description:
Said by: Anthony Loke, DAP Secretary-General / Transport Minister

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-03-09

📌 What Happened

DAP, the largest component party in Anwar’s Pakatan Harapan coalition by parliamentary seats, has brought forward its national congress from September to July 12, 2026. At the congress, more than 4,000 delegates will vote in an internal referendum on whether party leaders should resign from their government posts. DAP secretary-general and Transport Minister Anthony Loke told Malaysiakini on February 24 that “the government’s response has been inadequate and does not meet public expectations” regarding the MACC scandal. The move comes after DAP lost every seat it contested in November’s Sabah state polls and faces mounting grassroots anger over stalled reforms, the MACC crisis, and the perception that DAP is enabling a government that has abandoned its own platform.

item.title: Deputy Economy Minister Says Growth Steady at 4-4.5% — Amid a Regional War and Global Volatility
item.description:
Said by: Mohd Shahar Abdullah, Deputy Economy Minister

Category: Economy

Date: 2026-03-09

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: On 9 March 2026, Deputy Economy Minister Mohd Shahar Abdullah projected 4–4.5% GDP growth for Malaysia at the Asean Banking and Finance Summit, citing domestic consumption, infrastructure investment, the digital economy, and Visit Malaysia 2026. He made this statement at the very summit whose chairman, Tan Sri Majid Khan, warned that geopolitical tensions are “repricing risk across our entire region.” The US-Iran conflict was escalating, oil prices were spiking due to Strait of Hormuz disruptions, and GPS jamming was affecting shipping across the Gulf.

item.title: It's March 2026 — Where Is Anwar's Promise to Eradicate Hardcore Poverty by Q1?
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister and Finance Minister

Category: Economy

Date: 2026-03-09

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: On 7 December 2025, Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim publicly declared that Malaysia would eradicate hardcore poverty by the first quarter of 2026. Speaking at the closing of the Rancakkan Madani programme in Putrajaya, Anwar cited a hardcore poverty rate of 0.09% — roughly 7,000 households — and said the remaining cases could be resolved within months. “We are one of the countries in the world… we have managed to reduce the national rate of hardcore poverty to just 0.09 per cent,” Anwar said. “We can end it by the first quarter of 2026.” The promise was reported by Bernama, Malay Mail, The Star, New Straits Times, The Edge, and The Sun. It was a flagship claim — repeated during Budget 2026 and the Rancakkan Madani roadshow. It is now 9 March 2026. Q1 ends in three weeks. There has been no official announcement that hardcore poverty has been eradicated. No press conference. No Bernama flash. No triumphant PMO statement.

item.title: PKR's Own MP Warns Anwar Risks Legitimacy by Ignoring Nurul Izzah and Backbenchers on MACC
item.description:
Said by: Hassan Abdul Karim, PKR MP for Pasir Gudang

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-03-09

📌 What Happened

On 9 March 2026, PKR’s Pasir Gudang MP Hassan Abdul Karim publicly warned Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim — his own party president — that ignoring concerns raised by MPs and PKR deputy president Nurul Izzah Anwar about the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) puts the PM’s legitimacy at risk. Speaking to Malaysiakini, Hassan said Anwar cannot keep turning a deaf ear to internal party concerns about the MACC’s conduct, particularly after Nurul Izzah called for the agency to be placed under parliamentary oversight and criticised the “high-handed manner” of investigations including the RM1.1 billion Arm Holdings deal probe.

item.title: Sports Minister Says 'Learn Lessons and Move On' From Heritage Player Scandal — Where's the Government Accountability?
item.description:
Said by: Dr. Mohammed Taufiq Johari, Youth and Sports Minister

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-03-09

📌 WHAT HAPPENED

On March 9, 2026, Youth and Sports Minister Dr. Mohammed Taufiq Johari called on the Football Association of Malaysia (FAM) to “take lessons” from the heritage player scandal and move forward with grassroots football development.

“The legal process has taken place and we accept the decision that has been made. We will now examine what follow-up steps need to be taken,” Taufiq said at an Iftar Ramadan event in Tikam Batu.

item.title: The Ultimate Betrayal: Anwar Built Hope, Sold It to UMNO, and Called It Reform
item.description:
Said by: Compilation: CSIS, Toda, CNA, East Asia Forum, ISEAS, Amnesty, HRW, Freedom House

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-03-09

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: After three years in power, the verdict from international watchdogs, think tanks, and analysts is unanimous: Anwar Ibrahim has failed to deliver on his reform agenda.

  • CSIS: ‘The Death of Reformasi.’
  • Toda Peace Institute: ‘Making things worse.’
  • Amnesty International: ‘Betrayed reform pledges.’
  • HRW: ‘Reform agenda stalled.’
  • Freedom House: ‘Partly Free’ (unchanged).
  • CNA: ‘Perfect storm’ of crises.
  • East Asia Forum: ‘Reform aspirations clashed with political realities.’
  • ISEAS: ‘Needs rigour, fairness and self-awareness.’ 🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: This is not one critic, one perspective, or one partisan attack. This is a CONSENSUS across the world’s most respected institutions that Anwar’s reform project has failed. When CSIS in Washington, Amnesty in London, ISEAS in Singapore, Toda in Tokyo, and Freedom House all reach the same conclusion independently, it’s not bias — it’s the truth.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: Anwar had EVERYTHING: a personal narrative of suffering, a movement built on hope, a mandate from voters, parliamentary numbers, international goodwill, and decades of experience. He had more political capital than any incoming PM in Malaysian history. And he spent it ALL on maintaining power through UMNO’s patronage network rather than delivering the reforms that justified his entire political existence. This isn’t a failure of circumstance — it’s a failure of character.

item.title: Zafrul Says Malaysia 'Prepared' for Middle East Conflict Economic Fallout — More Vibes, No Plan
item.description:
Said by: Tengku Zafrul Abdul Aziz, Senior Political Advisor to PM

Category: Economy

Date: 2026-03-09

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: On 9 March 2026, Tengku Zafrul — now Senior Political Advisor to the PM — told media that the government is ‘prepared to face any eventuality affecting the country’s economic position’ from the Middle East conflict, including implications for petrol subsidies. He noted oil had previously reached US$100/barrel and said the Finance Ministry and Economy Ministry ‘have made preparations.’ He disclosed no specific preparations.

item.title: Zafrul Testifying in Muhyiddin Corruption Trial — The Man Who Served Both Sides Takes the Stand
item.description:
Said by: Tengku Zafrul Abdul Aziz / Muhyiddin Yassin Defence Team

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-03-09

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: Tengku Zafrul is scheduled to testify in the corruption trial of former PM Muhyiddin Yassin, who faces charges of soliciting RM232.5 million in bribes for Bersatu through the Jana Wibawa programme. Zafrul served as Muhyiddin’s Finance Minister during the period the alleged offences occurred (March 2020 to August 2021). The defence team has also signalled plans to call tycoon Syed Mokhtar to testify. Hearing dates are set through August 2026.

item.title: Anwar Signals Openness to PAS Engagement — The Man Who Ran Against Them Now Courts Them
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-03-08

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: On 8 March 2026, while defending his government’s RM15.5 billion anti-corruption recovery figure, Anwar Ibrahim simultaneously signalled openness to closer engagement with PAS — the Islamist party his coalition was built to oppose. This came as DAP was publicly reviewing its coalition ties and PH had just been wiped out in the Sabah state election, winning only 1 of 20 seats.

item.title: PKR Issues Show-Cause Letter to Rafizi, Then Backs Down — Party Can't Even Discipline Its Most Prominent Critic
item.description:
Said by: Rafizi Ramli, Pandan MP / Fuziah Salleh, PKR Secretary-General

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-03-08

📌 What Happened

On February 19, Pandan MP Rafizi Ramli revealed he had received a show-cause letter from PKR secretary-general Fuziah Salleh after telling Sin Chew Daily he would contest in Pandan at GE16 but likely not as a PKR candidate. Rafizi responded saying the meaning of his comments was “understandable to anyone with a good command of Malay” and that he was free to make his own plans after Parliament was dissolved. On March 6, PKR conveyed its decision by email — no action taken, just a reminder to adhere to the party constitution and code of ethics. On March 8, Rafizi publicly described this as a “U-turn,” saying the show-cause letter was issued “with the intention to give him the boot” but the party ultimately blinked.

item.title: Teresa Kok Says Coalition Unity More Important Than RCI Into MACC — DAP's Own MP Undermines the Accountability Push
item.description:
Said by: Teresa Kok, MP for Seputeh (DAP)

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-03-08

📌 What Happened

On March 8, DAP’s Seputeh MP Teresa Kok publicly stated that maintaining unity within the Madani coalition government is more important than her own party’s push for a Royal Commission of Inquiry (RCI) into the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC). This came just days after DAP secretary-general Anthony Loke told Malaysiakini the government’s response to MACC misconduct allegations had been “inadequate” and called for an independent inquiry — a position endorsed by DAP’s national leadership and reinforced by the party’s decision to bring forward its special congress to July 2026.

item.title: Anwar Warns of 'Wealthy Elites' Manipulating Media — While His Own Government Shields the MACC Chief From Accountability
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-03-07

📌 What Happened

On March 7, 2026, Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim used an iftar event in Kulim to accuse unnamed Malaysian elites of “corruptly using their wealth to exert influence on international media, online narratives and local politics.” He said he was not referring only to the opposition but to “everyone,” and urged Malaysians to “remain vigilant” against “foreign schemes and conspiracies.” The statement came just four days after he told Parliament about an alleged plot — involving a former senior minister’s wife, foreign PR firms, and Bloomberg — to destabilise his government.

item.title: Anwar's 'Perfect Storm': Anti-Graft Crisis, Party Fractures, Coalition Jitters
item.description:
Said by: CNA Analysis

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-03-07

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: Multiple challenges are converging on Anwar Ibrahim that may force him to call early elections nearly two years ahead of the February 2028 deadline. An anti-graft crisis at MACC (Bloomberg exposés on Azam Baki), PKR internal fractures following Rafizi Ramli’s departure from cabinet, and coalition partners openly reconsidering their ties. CNA described it as a “perfect storm” of political instability. UMNO’s secret Bangkok meeting with opposition parties, DAP reviewing coalition ties, and the PKR factional war all threaten to bring down the Unity Government before its term ends.

item.title: Rafizi Tells PH Voters to 'Wait Till June' — Hints at Breaking Away from PKR and 'Sailing His Own Ship'
item.description:
Said by: Rafizi Ramli, Pandan MP / Former Economy Minister

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-03-07

📌 What Happened

Former Economy Minister Rafizi Ramli, who quit the cabinet in May 2025 after losing the PKR deputy presidency contest, told Pakatan Harapan supporters in a Sin Chew Daily interview to “wait till June,” hinting that a new political option would be unveiled. Rafizi said he and his allies are open to “sailing their own ship” and are exploring “different political configurations” — which could involve a new party, coalition, or political platform. He confirmed he will defend his Pandan parliamentary seat in the next general election but definitively not under the PKR banner. Separately, Reuters reported that several PKR lawmakers are considering contesting as independents, having lost confidence in Anwar’s leadership.

item.title: 238 Political Appointments to FSBs and GLCs Under Anwar — IDEAS Report Exposes 'Connections Over Competence'
item.description:
Said by: IDEAS Report / Anwar Ibrahim Administration

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-03-06

📌 What Happened

The Institute for Democracy and Economic Affairs (IDEAS) released a report titled Who Sits at the Table: From Political to Public Appointees in Malaysia’s Federal Statutory Bodies on March 6, 2026, revealing that 238 political appointments were made to federal statutory bodies (FSBs) and government-linked companies (GLCs) under Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim’s administration between November 2022 and October 2025. Of these, 222 were to FSBs and 16 to GLCs. The report reviewed 135 agencies and found that 78% of chairperson positions were filled based on political connections rather than competence. Most lacked clearly defined qualification criteria. The appointments were concentrated in key ministries, and the governance provisions around them were described as inconsistent and fragmented.

item.title: Anwar Admits He Can Only Hold RON95 Subsidy for 'One or Two Months' — While Fuel Prices Spike Across the Board
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister and Finance Minister

Category: Economy

Date: 2026-03-06

📌 What Happened

On March 6, 2026, Prime Minister and Finance Minister Anwar Ibrahim told reporters that Malaysia can maintain the subsidised RON95 petrol price at RM1.99 per litre under the Budi95 scheme — but only for ‘one or two months.’ His exact words: ‘We will try to control the effects of the Iranian conflict, including for RON95, which is RM1.99 per litre. We will monitor this situation because so far, we can still hold off for one or two months.’ This came the same week that RON97 petrol rose 10 sen to RM3.25 per litre, unsubsidised RON95 climbed 8 sen to RM2.67, and diesel in Peninsular Malaysia jumped 8 sen to RM3.12 — all driven by the escalating Middle East conflict and 200 vessels stuck in the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Anwar Boasts RM426.7B 'Record' Approved Investments — But 'Approved' Doesn't Mean 'Arrived'
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister and Finance Minister

Category: Economy

Date: 2026-03-06

📌 WHAT HAPPENED

On March 6, 2026, Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim announced that Malaysia recorded its highest-ever approved investments of RM426.7 billion in 2025 — an 11% increase from RM384.4 billion in 2024. According to MIDA, 8,390 projects were approved, with the potential to create 244,902 jobs. Anwar framed this as a vindication of his reform agenda and evidence of global confidence in Malaysia’s economic direction.

item.title: Anwar Denied Snap Polls in December — Now His Government Is Quietly Preparing for Elections in 2026
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister / Unity Government sources

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-03-06

📌 What Happened

In December 2025, Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim told journalists that snap elections were not on the cards — his priority, he said, was implementing reforms and addressing cost of living. Fast forward to March 2026: multiple government insiders, party grassroots leaders, and civil servants have told CNA that preparations are quietly underway for a possible general election in the second half of 2026. A Johor politician from the unity government said grassroots have been told to ramp up preparations after Hari Raya Aidilfitri. A senior civil servant confirmed government administrative officers have been placed on alert and resources put “on standby” from June. EC deputy chairman Azmi Sharom said the commission is ready, including for concurrent state and parliamentary polls.

item.title: Anwar Tells Bersih to Stop Criticising MACC 'During Ramadan' — Uses the Holy Month as a Shield Against Accountability
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-03-06

📌 What Happened

On March 6, 2026, Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim publicly dismissed the Coalition for Clean and Fair Elections (Bersih)’s accusation that the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) has become a political tool of the government. Speaking after the Public Service Department’s Ihya’ Ramadan programme in Putrajaya, Anwar called Bersih’s statement “unfair” and said the group was “not making clean statements.” He then added: “Do not attack people without first obtaining information, especially during Ramadan.” He pointed to the government’s investigative committee and the Attorney General as reasons to defer criticism, saying no reports had been received yet.

item.title: DAP Reviews Coalition Ties Over MACC Scandal — Unity Government Cracking
item.description:
Said by: DAP / Reuters

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-03-05

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: On 5 March 2026, DAP — the largest non-Malay party in Anwar’s ruling coalition — publicly announced it was “reviewing its role” in the Unity Government amid deepening rifts over the MACC scandal. DAP called for an independent inquiry into MACC’s alleged “corporate mafia” operations, saying the government’s task force “does not go far enough.” The announcement came after weeks of Bloomberg exposés about MACC chief Azam Baki’s shareholdings and alleged misconduct.

item.title: DAPSY Chairman Warns MACC Creating 'Climate of Fear' — PH's Own Youth Wing Breaks Ranks on Enforcement Tactics
item.description:
Said by: Woo Kah Leong, DAPSY National Chairman

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-03-05

📌 WHAT HAPPENED

On 5 March 2026, DAPSY National Chairman YB Woo Kah Leong issued a formal seven-point press statement criticising the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) for conducting what he called disproportionate and intimidating investigations.

The key points of the statement:

  • DAPSY is “deeply concerned” about how MACC has conducted recent high-profile investigations
  • The individual being investigated is “neither a fugitive financier like Jho Low nor a powerful businessman linked to corporate mafia allegations”
  • The high-profile nature of the investigation “appears disproportionate and unnecessary”
  • MACC “must not engage in political witch-hunting against an ordinary citizen”
  • If enforcement powers are exercised selectively, “the Unity Government will inevitably bear the political consequences”
  • Anti-corruption efforts must “strengthen democracy and ensure good governance, instead of weakening it by creating the perception that government machinery is used to pressure or silence individuals” The statement is widely understood to refer to MACC’s search for a former aide to ex-Economy Minister Rafizi Ramli, identified in various reports as Chai. The MACC had launched a high-profile search operation that drew public attention and criticism.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS

This is not an opposition attack. This is DAP’s own youth wing — a key component of the ruling Pakatan Harapan coalition — publicly accusing the government’s anti-corruption agency of creating a “climate of fear” and engaging in “political witch-hunting.”

item.title: Fahmi Blames 'Opposition Confusion' for PM Tenure Bill Failure — When 8 of His OWN MPs Didn't Show Up
item.description:
Said by: Fahmi Fadzil, Communications Minister

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-03-05

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: On March 5, 2026, Communications Minister Fahmi Fadzil told reporters in Putrajaya that the constitutional amendment bill to limit the PM’s tenure to 10 years failed because opposition MPs were “confused.” He claimed several opposition lawmakers mistakenly believed the bill was designed to give PM Anwar Ibrahim a 10-year term in office, rather than to cap any future PM’s tenure. Fahmi said: “There were MPs from the opposition who gave feedback to one of the ministers saying they thought the vote that day was meant to give Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim a 10-year term as prime minister.” He did not identify which opposition MPs were allegedly confused. PAS secretary-general Takiyuddin Hassan hit back, calling the claim “irresponsible” and “misplaced.”

item.title: PKR Accused of Lobbying US Against Najib — The SAME Thing Anwar Calls a 'Plot' When Done to Him
item.description:
Said by: Shahidan Kassim (PN-Arau) / Malaysiakini Parliament Report

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-03-05

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: In parliament, opposition MP Shahidan Kassim confronted Anwar Ibrahim with allegations that PKR had communicated with a US firm a decade ago to exert pressure on then-PM Najib Razak. Shahidan demanded Anwar explain the double standard: PKR lobbied foreign powers against Najib, but when Daim Zainuddin’s family allegedly engaged international PR firms, Anwar calls it a ‘plot to topple the government’ and launches police investigations.

item.title: AG-Public Prosecutor Separation Bill Postponed for 3-Month Committee Review — Reform Delayed Again
item.description:
Said by: Fahmi Fadzil, Communications Minister

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-03-04

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: On 4 March 2026, the Constitution (Amendment) Bill to separate the roles of Attorney General and Public Prosecutor was referred to a parliamentary special select committee for three more months of review instead of being debated. Communications Minister Fahmi Fadzil confirmed the timeline. This is a cornerstone PH reform promise — pledged since GE14 in 2018 — still not passed after more than three years in government. Bersih called for the committee to conduct a thorough review including mechanisms for parliamentary and judicial oversight.

item.title: MACC Investigates Rafizi Over RM1.1 Billion ARM Holdings Deal — His Aide Flees to UK
item.description:
Said by: Rafizi Ramli, Former Economy Minister; Azam Baki, MACC Chief

Category: Economy

Date: 2026-03-04

📌 WHAT HAPPENED

In February-March 2026, MACC launched a formal investigation into a RM1.1 billion government deal with UK chip giant ARM Holdings, signed in March 2025 during Rafizi Ramli’s tenure as Economy Minister. The deal involved Malaysia Investment Development Authority (MIDA) obtaining intellectual property licences and compute subsystems valued at US$250 million, payable over 10 years.

item.title: Nearly RM1 Billion Paid in Court Damages for Government Negligence — Anwar's Fix? Letters of Reprimand
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister and Finance Minister

Category: Economy

Date: 2026-03-04

📌 What Happened

Prime Minister and Finance Minister Anwar Ibrahim disclosed in a written parliamentary reply — responding to PH’s own William Leong (Selayang) — that the Malaysian government paid out RM933,360,633 in court-awarded damages between 2020 and 2025. The payouts were the result of negligence, misconduct, and other wrongdoing by government officials and agencies. Anwar outlined measures including instructing ministries to conduct investigations into the cases and “recommend disciplinary action” against those found negligent — and, spectacularly, issuing letters of reprimand.

item.title: Negri Sembilan MB Admits His Driver Runs Red Lights — 'I Have No Choice, Look at My Facebook Schedule'
item.description:
Said by: Aminuddin Harun, Menteri Besar of Negri Sembilan (PH-Lenggeng)

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-03-04

📌 What Happened

On March 4, 2026, Negri Sembilan Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun publicly admitted that his official car routinely runs red lights, after a viral video showed a vehicle believed to be his using blue strobe lights and a siren to blast through a red light on Jalan Rasah. Speaking to reporters after a state executive council meeting, Aminuddin said: “I admit that we have to beat the light at times, but I have no choice, as there are sometimes several official events lined up in a day.” He pointed to his Facebook page as proof of his hectic schedule. He added that a police officer is always present in the car, and that his office has sought “necessary approvals from the relevant authorities.”

item.title: 5G Dual Network Fiasco — DNB vs TM Contract War Over Termination
item.description:
Said by: Digital Nasional Bhd / Telekom Malaysia

Category: Economy

Date: 2026-03-03

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: On 3 March 2026, Telekom Malaysia (TM) attempted to terminate its 5G wholesale agreement with Digital Nasional Berhad (DNB), the government entity managing Malaysia’s 5G rollout. DNB rejected the termination, saying the contract runs until 2032 and remains binding. Malaysia’s dual 5G network strategy — introduced after Anwar’s government reversed the original single-network plan — has been plagued by policy flip-flops, RM2 billion in DNB debt, and the controversial awarding of the second 5G network to U Mobile.

item.title: Anwar Invokes 'Zionists' and 'Bankers' in Parliament to Smear Daim's Widow — Lawyers Call It an 'Outright Lie'
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-03-03

📌 What Happened

On March 3, Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim told the Dewan Rakyat that the alleged plot to destabilise his government — attributed to Na’imah Abdul Khalid, the widow of former Finance Minister Daim Zainuddin — involved “prominent Zionist groups” and international bankers. He claimed a UK-based PR firm had been engaged to launch a coordinated attack on national institutions. This came one day after MalaysiaNow revealed that PKR leaders, including figures close to Anwar, had themselves engaged a US consultant in 2016 to build White House pressure on then-PM Najib Razak. Na’imah’s lawyers, Rajesh Nagarajan and Sachpreetraj Singh, issued a statement the same day calling Anwar’s claims “false, preposterous, outrageous, and a blatant attempt to mislead Parliament.”

item.title: Lynas Rare Earth License Renewed 10 Years — Radioactive Waste Still in Pahang
item.description:
Said by: Chang Lih Kang, Science Minister

Category: Other

Date: 2026-03-03

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: On 3 March 2026, Science Minister Chang Lih Kang confirmed the renewal of Lynas Rare Earth’s operating licence for 10 years, despite ongoing concerns about radioactive waste storage. Greenpeace Malaysia warned that the country risks becoming a “pollution hub.” Environmental groups said government claims about thorium extraction from the waste are “scientifically unsound.” Close to a million tonnes of radioactive waste from Lynas’s processing operations remain stored in Pahang, with no permanent disposal solution in place.

item.title: Anwar Condemns US-Israel Strikes on Iran in Parliament — 'We Can No Longer Swallow' Western Human Rights Lectures, PH MP Calls for US Embassy Boycott
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister / Syahredzan Johan (PH-Bangi)

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-03-02

📌 WHAT HAPPENED

On 2 March 2026, Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim tabled an emergency motion in the Dewan Rakyat condemning the US and Israel’s strikes on Iran, which began on 28 February and killed at least 201 people including Iran’s Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.

Anwar called the strikes a “flagrant violation of international law” and described Western lectures on human rights and democracy as hypocrisy:

item.title: PKR Hired US Consultants to Lobby Washington Against Najib — Now Cries Foul When Others Do the Same
item.description:
Said by: Sivarasa Rasiah, Former PKR Vice-President (confirmed); PKR leadership

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-03-02

📌 WHAT HAPPENED

In March 2026, MalaysiaNow published leaked emails revealing that PKR had engaged a Washington-based political consultancy firm in 2016 to lobby the United States to exert pressure on then-Prime Minister Najib Razak. The emails included an 84-page dossier making serious allegations against Najib, with the goal of getting Washington to publicly rebuke the Malaysian government.

item.title: Anwar Is Running Mahathir's Playbook 2.0 — Different Man, Same Authoritarian Instincts
item.description:
Said by: Political Analysts / ISEAS / Toda Peace Institute / CSIS

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-03-01

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: Multiple independent analyses from ISEAS, CSIS, Toda Peace Institute, and East Asia Forum converge on the same conclusion: Anwar Ibrahim’s governance style increasingly mirrors that of Mahathir Mohamad — the man who imprisoned him. Centralisation of power (PM+FM), use of repressive laws, crackdown on media, selective prosecution, race-based politics, foreign policy grandstanding as domestic distraction, and coalition management through patronage.

item.title: The Scoreboard: Things That Were BETTER Under BN Than PH — An Uncomfortable Truth
item.description:
Said by: Comparative Economic Data / Various Sources

Category: Economy

Date: 2026-03-01

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: By multiple measurable metrics, ordinary Malaysians were objectively better off under certain BN policies than under PH: (1) GST was more efficient and less regressive than SST; (2) Diesel was RM2.15 vs RM3.35; (3) Festive toll-free periods existed; (4) PISA scores were 31 points higher in math; (5) Food prices were lower; (6) RON95 subsidy was universal without the anxiety of ’targeted’ reform; (7) Electricity for domestic use was GST-exempt.

item.title: Azam Baki's RM100M Defamation Suit Against Bloomberg — MACC Chief Fights Media Instead of Corruption
item.description:
Said by: Bloomberg / Azam Baki, MACC Chief Commissioner

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-02-27

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: On 27 February 2026, it was reported that MACC Chief Commissioner Azam Baki filed a RM100 million defamation suit against Bloomberg. The suit came after Bloomberg published an investigation alleging that Azam owned millions of shares in publicly listed companies — potentially breaching civil servant ownership limits. Bloomberg further alleged that MACC officials had colluded with businessmen to orchestrate hostile takeovers of listed firms, using raids and intimidation to force executives into selling shares to groups dubbed the “corporate mafia.” Rather than resigning or submitting to an independent investigation, Azam chose to sue the messenger. Anwar’s response was to announce a task force — not an independent inquiry, not a Royal Commission. Even DAP, his own coalition partner, said a task force “does not go far enough.”

item.title: Every Cabinet Member Meeting KPIs, Says Anwar — Critics Ask: What KPIs?
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-02-25

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: On 25 February 2026, Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim told Parliament that every cabinet member is meeting their KPIs and performing with integrity. He did not disclose what those KPIs were, how they were measured, or who evaluated them. Anwar was responding to an opposition MP who asked if ministers would be forced to resign for poor performance. His reply: “Accountability is not only about punishment but also correction, continuous improvement, and the moral responsibility ministers held for the people.” No KPIs have ever been publicly disclosed for any minister in Anwar’s cabinet. No minister has been penalised, demoted, or publicly reprimanded for underperformance. Two ministers were removed in the December 2025 reshuffle — including Rafizi Ramli — but Anwar framed it as “portfolio realignment,” not performance accountability.

item.title: MACC 'Corporate Mafia' Allegations — DAP Demands Independent Inquiry, Anwar Resists
item.description:
Said by: DAP Leaders / SCMP

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-02-25

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: In late February 2026, DAP leaders publicly stated that Anwar’s proposed task force to examine alleged misconduct at MACC “does not go far enough.” They demanded an independent inquiry instead. SCMP reported that the push came after Bloomberg’s investigation alleged MACC officials had colluded with businessmen to orchestrate hostile takeovers of listed firms — the so-called “corporate mafia” allegations. Anwar’s response — a government-controlled task force rather than an independent commission — put his “reform agenda under renewed scrutiny.” Even his own coalition allies don’t trust his anti-corruption mechanisms.

item.title: MACC in Crisis with No Credible Leadership — The Anti-Corruption Watchdog Has Become the Scandal
item.description:
Said by: DAP / Bloomberg / CNA Analysis

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-02-25

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) is embroiled in its worst-ever credibility crisis. Chief Commissioner Azam Baki faces Bloomberg allegations of owning millions in shares (breaching civil servant limits) and colluding with a ‘corporate mafia.’ Rather than resign, Azam filed a RM100 million defamation suit against Bloomberg. DAP, PH’s own coalition partner, has demanded an independent inquiry, saying the government’s task force ‘does not go far enough.’ CNA called it part of Anwar’s ‘perfect storm.’

item.title: Penang Exco Sacked After Charged for Assaulting Wife — How Did DAP Vet This Person?
item.description:
Said by: Chow Kon Yeow / Fahmi / Penang DAP

Category: Social

Date: 2026-02-24

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: On 24 February 2026, a Penang executive councillor was detained for allegedly assaulting his wife. He contacted Chow Kon Yeow to apologise. He was subsequently charged in court and sacked from his exco position. The incident drew attention to DAP’s leadership vetting processes — how a state-level cabinet member with alleged domestic violence issues was appointed to a senior position.

item.title: Rawang Hindu Temple Demolished at Midnight — No Court Order, Police Assisted, PH State Government Silent
item.description:
Said by: Selangor PH State Government / Police

Category: Social

Date: 2026-02-24

📌 WHAT HAPPENED

On 24 February 2026, the Sri Uchimalai Muniswaran temple in Rawang, Selangor was demolished in the middle of the night — hours after deities were allegedly relocated following pressure on the temple committee. The demolition was carried out with police assistance and without any court order.

Lawyers Rajesh Nagarajan and Sachpreetraj Singh issued a statement: “We strongly deny the claims by Yayasan Kubra representative Ariff Omar that the temple committee voluntarily removed the deities. That is false and ridiculous since the temple chairman lodged a police report only yesterday complaining that the temple was being pressured to vacate the land without any court order.”

item.title: Zafrul vs Rafizi — Former MITI Minister Tells Rafizi to 'Stop Spinning' Over Double Tariff Claims
item.description:
Said by: Tengku Zafrul Abdul Aziz, MIDA Chairman / former MITI Minister

Category: Economy

Date: 2026-02-23

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: On 23 February 2026, Tengku Zafrul — now MIDA chairman — publicly rebuked former Economy Minister Rafizi Ramli for claiming Malaysia faced nearly double the tariff rate under the US trade deal. Zafrul accused Rafizi of ‘distorting the facts’ and told him to ‘stop spinning — it’s exhausting to keep responding.’ Rafizi had argued the ART locked Malaysia into unfavourable terms. Zafrul countered that the 19% tariff no longer applied after the US Supreme Court struck down Trump’s tariff framework.

item.title: Rafizi Alleges Anwar Is Fueling Social Media Attacks Against Him — Rift Goes Public
item.description:
Said by: Rafizi Ramli, former Economy Minister

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-02-22

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: On 22 February 2026, former Economy Minister Rafizi Ramli publicly alleged that Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim was behind coordinated social media campaigns targeting him. Business Today reported Rafizi accused Anwar of “fueling his social media influence” — using coordinated accounts to attack his credibility and undermine his political standing within PKR. SCMP followed up on 18 February, framing Rafizi as seizing “the banner of reform and clean government that was once Anwar’s own” and asking whether he was now “the conscience of the Reformasi movement.” Anwar dismissed the rift as a “distraction.” Rafizi had previously warned Anwar that the CPI flatline at 50 points was “extremely dangerous” for the government’s credibility. His reward: being sidelined from cabinet in the December 2025 reshuffle. The Economy Ministry portfolio sat vacant for six months after his departure.

item.title: DAP Special Congress July 12 — Party Questions Its Own Role in Government While Staying in Government
item.description:
Said by: Chow Kon Yeow, Penang Chief Minister / DAP

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-02-21

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: On 21 February 2026, Penang CM Chow Kon Yeow announced that DAP would hold a special congress on 12 July 2026 to decide the party’s role in Anwar’s government. Chow insisted DAP was ’not withdrawing support from the Madani government’ but was discussing ‘whether to continue holding ministerial posts and certain positions.’ DAP’s 40 MPs would continue supporting the government. All members, including leaders, must abide by the congress decision.

item.title: MAHB Delisted After GDA Takeover — Five Directors Resign in Protest
item.description:
Said by: Bursa Malaysia / GDA Consortium

Category: Economy

Date: 2026-02-18

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: On 18 February 2026, Malaysia Airports Holdings Berhad (MAHB) — the public airport operator managing 39 airports nationwide — was officially delisted from Bursa Malaysia after the GDA consortium completed its takeover at RM11 per share. The consortium was led by Khazanah Nasional and EPF, with BlackRock’s Global Infrastructure Partners (GIP) as a key partner. All five independent directors resigned in protest, having deemed the offer “unfair and unreasonable” to minority shareholders. Hong Leong Investment Bank’s independent assessment concluded the offer was “not fair but reasonable” — a diplomatic way of saying shareholders were getting shortchanged but had no better option. Civil society groups protested BlackRock’s GIP involvement amid Palestine boycott concerns.

item.title: Unity Coalition 'Faltering' — Reform Mandate Unmet, Power Struggles Naked
item.description:
Said by: UCA News Analysis

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-02-15

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: ‘Malaysia’s Unity Government is beginning to look less like a grand coalition forged in the national interest and more like a fragile ceasefire among parties that no longer trust one another.’ PH campaigned on clear, specific, publicly measurable pledges — most remain unmet.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: When even sympathetic international observers describe your coalition as ‘faltering,’ the narrative has shifted from ‘can they deliver?’ to ‘how long can they survive?’ The unity government was sold as a necessary compromise to keep extremists out. But compromise without delivery is just power-sharing for its own sake.

item.title: DAP Internal War: Chow vs Guan Eng Over Penang Councillor Appointments
item.description:
Said by: Chow Kon Yeow / Lim Guan Eng / Penang DAP

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-02-14

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: Penang DAP was forced to publicly clarify an ‘alleged misunderstanding’ between CM Chow Kon Yeow and his predecessor Lim Guan Eng over the appointment of councillors for both city councils. DAP publicity secretary Joshua Woo insisted the appointments followed ‘required conditions and processes’ and were consistent with ‘institutional precedent.’ The statement was damage control after reports suggested a factional power struggle between Chow’s camp and Guan Eng’s allies over who controls appointments in Penang.

item.title: Anwar Defends MACC Chief Azam Baki Over Second Shareholding Scandal, Tells Critics to 'Read His Explanation'
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-02-12

📌 WHAT HAPPENED

In February 2026, Bloomberg reported that MACC chief Azam Baki was a key shareholder in a financial services company — Velocity Capital Partners Berhad — based on corporate filings. This was Azam’s second shareholding scandal, following the 2022 controversy over shares in Excel Force MSC Berhad.

Civil society groups including C4 Center demanded Azam’s immediate suspension pending investigation. They noted concerning links between Velocity Capital Partners and MMAG Holdings, where controversial former Anwar aide Farhash Wafa Salvador had acquired a 20% stake and was appointed chairman.

item.title: Selangor Bans All Pig Farming — Amirudin Caves to Sultan's Decree, Chinese Farmers Left With Nothing
item.description:
Said by: Amirudin Shari, Selangor Menteri Besar

Category: Social

Date: 2026-02-12

📌 WHAT HAPPENED

On 12 February 2026, Selangor Menteri Besar Amirudin Shari announced that the state government has stopped issuing pig farming licences and will accelerate the closure of all existing pig farms. The decision followed a decree from Sultan Sharafuddin Idris Shah, who declared that no pig farms should operate in any district in Selangor.

An earlier plan to relocate pig farms to Bukit Tagar — a sparsely populated area in northern Selangor — was shelved after Malay residents objected due to proximity to Sungai Selangor, a major water treatment source. Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim intervened in January to halt the relocation.

item.title: Tiong King Sing Pulls 'Do You Know Who I Am?' Card While Lecturing Staff About Rudeness
item.description:
Said by: Tiong King Sing, Tourism, Arts and Culture Minister

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-02-12

📌 WHAT HAPPENED

On 12 February 2026, Tourism Minister Tiong King Sing launched the ‘Mesra Malaysia’ (Friendly Malaysia) campaign at KL Sentral — a campaign about being welcoming and courteous to tourists. During the launch, Tiong complained about rudeness from public and private sector employees, saying: “Some officers think they have a lot of power. But when people don’t understand certain issues, we must explain.”

item.title: Nga Kor Ming's MyKiosk Project: No Needs Assessment, No KPIs, MACC Calls It a Governance Failure
item.description:
Said by: Nga Kor Ming, Housing and Local Government Minister

Category: Housing

Date: 2026-02-10

📌 WHAT HAPPENED

On 10 February 2026, the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) revealed that the Housing and Local Government Ministry (KPKT) under Nga Kor Ming failed to carry out a proper needs assessment before launching the MyKiosk initiative — a nationwide programme to provide traders with modern kiosks.

MACC issued five governance improvement recommendations and stated: “The absence of a needs assessment and a comprehensive outcome-monitoring mechanism, including KPIs, hindered the evaluation of the project’s impact at the local authority level.”

item.title: Students Flash Red Cards at Adam Adli Over Broken UUCA Promise — PH's Own Poster Boy Turns Reneger
item.description:
Said by: Adam Adli Abdul Halim, Deputy Higher Education Minister

Category: Education

Date: 2026-02-09

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: On 9 February 2026, university students marched to Parliament demanding the abolition of the Universities and University Colleges Act 1971 (UUCA). Their target? Deputy Higher Education Minister Adam Adli Abdul Halim — once Malaysia’s most famous student activist, now the living embodiment of PH’s broken promise. Abolishing UUCA was a key manifesto promise by Pakatan Harapan in both GE14 (2018) and GE15 (2022). In late January 2026, Higher Education Minister Zambry Abd Kadir told Parliament the government had “no plans” to abolish UUCA — a policy reversal delivered without fanfare. Students flashed red cards at Adam Adli and called him a “reneger.” Zambry refused to receive their memorandum. Adam Adli, 33, was arrested in 2013 for lowering a flag at a student rally — an act that made him a symbol of the student movement. PH championed his case as proof that UUCA was unjust. Now he sits in the ministry that enforces it.

item.title: Fahmi Fadzil: Free Speech Doesn't Mean Freedom to Offend Online
item.description:
Said by: Fahmi Fadzil, Communications Minister

Category: Social

Date: 2026-02-07

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: On 7 February 2026, Communications Minister Fahmi Fadzil told the public that free speech does not mean freedom to offend or defame online. This came after the Federal Court reinstated Section 233 of the Communications and Multimedia Act (CMA), which criminalises content deemed “grossly offensive” — a provision PH once opposed. Fahmi said the 2024 amendments raised the threshold from “offensive” to “grossly offensive,” claiming this made it harder to misuse. The Online Safety Act 2025 (ONSA) took effect on 1 January 2026, giving MCMC powers to penalise platforms up to RM10 million for failing to remove harmful content. MCMC — the body that decides what is “grossly offensive” — reports directly to Fahmi’s ministry.

item.title: MACC Probes Senior Penang State Official Over Land Deal — Chow Says 'We Are Not Investigators'
item.description:
Said by: Chow Kon Yeow, Penang Chief Minister

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-01-31

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: On 31 January 2026, Penang CM Chow Kon Yeow confirmed that MACC was investigating allegations of abuse of power involving a senior state official over a land deal. Chow said the state government would ‘give full space’ to MACC and would not conduct its own internal probe. ‘We are not investigators, but we will provide full cooperation,’ he told reporters. The official was reportedly continuing to perform duties as usual during the investigation.

item.title: River Water Quality Declining — More Polluted Rivers in 2025 Than 2024
item.description:
Said by: Natural Resources Ministry

Category: Other

Date: 2026-01-28

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: According to data released by the Natural Resources Ministry in late January 2026, Malaysia’s river water quality deteriorated year-over-year. Clean rivers dropped from 486 to 468. “Moderate” quality rivers rose from 161 to 171. “Slightly polluted” rivers jumped from 25 to 33. The numbers are moving in the wrong direction across every category. This decline occurred as the government aggressively pushed rare earth mining expansion — one site in Perak already turned a river visibly blue from mining discharges — and continued to promote Malaysia as a “green economy” leader.

item.title: Malaysia Study: Cost-Benefit Analysis of US Trade Deal Shows 'Concerns' But Government Says Don't Quit
item.description:
Said by: Malaysian Government

Category: Economy

Date: 2026-01-27

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: In January 2026, Bloomberg reported that the Malaysian government announced a formal cost-benefit analysis of the controversial US-Malaysia Agreed Reciprocal Trade (ART) agreement — following months of mounting criticism from former AG Tommy Thomas (who called it “the worst agreement since independence”), former PM Mahathir, think tanks, and opposition politicians. But in the same breath, the government warned lawmakers not to quit the pact. East Asia Forum published a detailed analysis titled “The Art of the Deal Lost in US-Malaysia Pact,” arguing Malaysia surrendered critical mineral export controls, digital services taxation rights, and independent trade policy in exchange for a tariff reduction from 25% to 19% — still punitive by any standard. The deal’s details were first published on the White House website before being shared with Malaysian Parliament.

item.title: Selangor's Illegal Waste Dumping Crisis — Years of Promises, Zero Results Under Amirudin
item.description:
Said by: Amirudin Shari, Selangor Menteri Besar

Category: Social

Date: 2026-01-26

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: On 26 January 2026, Amirudin Shari declared ’no more finger pointing’ and ordered a ’total crackdown’ on illegal waste dumping across Selangor. He said he would personally visit sites with local authorities and ’take firm action.’ He instructed the Land Office and local authorities to focus on cleanliness. This follows a similar declaration in early 2026 where the Selangor government announced intensified efforts to ’eradicate’ illegal waste dumping.

item.title: Zulkifli Hasan Says Work Stress Can Make People Gay — International Community Laughs, Malaysia Cringes
item.description:
Said by: Zulkifli Hasan, Minister in PM’s Department (Religious Affairs)

Category: Social

Date: 2026-01-26

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: On January 26, 2026, Religious Affairs Minister Zulkifli Hasan submitted a written parliamentary reply to PAS MP Siti Zailah Mohd Yusoff about LGBT trends in Malaysia. He cited a 2017 study and listed “societal influence, sexual experiences, work stress, and other personal factors” as possible causes of “LGBT behaviour.” The “work stress” claim went immediately viral — globally. CNA, SCMP, NDTV, Reuters, Hindustan Times, LGBTQ Nation, and them.us all covered it. Malaysians flooded social media with mockery, sarcastically asking whether parliament MPs had “turned gay” since they apparently never work hard enough to experience stress. Zulkifli also disclosed that from 2022 to 2025, 135 cases related to “LGBT activities” were recorded as arrests or prosecutions — in a country where same-sex relations remain criminalised.

item.title: Mandatory Malay and History Across ALL Education Systems — Critics Say It Stifles Diversity
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister

Category: Education

Date: 2026-01-21

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: In January 2026, Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim announced that Bahasa Melayu and History would be mandatory across all education systems in Malaysia — including vernacular schools (SJKC and SJKT), international schools, and private institutions. Anwar said the requirement aims to “bolster the status of Bahasa Melayu as the national language” and provide “a common understanding of national history.” Critics argued this undermines constitutional protections for vernacular education under Article 152(1)(a) and stifles the diversity that makes Malaysia’s education system unique. CNA’s in-depth analysis noted the “politics and difficulties” of Malaysian education reform, describing how “language and religion” remain the most combustible policy areas.

item.title: FMT Journalist Rex Tan Arrested Under Sedition Act for Asking a 'Sensitive Question'
item.description:
Said by: Royal Malaysia Police

Category: Social

Date: 2026-01-17

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: In January 2026, Free Malaysia Today journalist Rex Tan was arrested at dawn under the colonial-era Sedition Act 1948 for asking a question at a public debate that linked the Gaza crisis to the treatment of ethnic Chinese in Malaysia. Police arrived at Tan’s home early on a Saturday morning — five days after the debate — and arrested him under Section 4(1) of the Sedition Act. The question was provocative but fell squarely within the bounds of legitimate public discourse. SCMP reported the arrest “casts doubts on Anwar’s reform record.” International press freedom organisations, including RSF and CPJ, condemned the arrest. Over 90 sedition-related arrests have occurred since Anwar took power in late 2022.

item.title: Anwar Calls for 'Patience and Wisdom' After Every Crisis — But Never Accountability
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-01-15

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: Across every major crisis from 2024 to 2026, Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim’s response has followed the same template: acknowledge the issue, call for “patience and wisdom,” dismiss critics, and move on without structural change. When Zahid’s 47 charges were dropped: “I don’t interfere in AG decisions.” When Shamsul Iskandar was caught issuing support letters: “The letters were vague” — reprimand only. When Najib’s house arrest bid was raised: “Respond with patience and wisdom.” When Bersih criticised MACC: “Unfair” — told them to wait. When diesel prices hiked: “We will be criticised with slander and lies.” When KK Mart stores were firebombed: “Don’t be prosecutor or judge.” When PH was wiped out in Sabah: “Defeat won’t stop delivery.” When Rafizi accused him of social media attacks: called it a “distraction.”

item.title: Palestine Solidarity But Silence on Uyghurs — Double Standards Exposed
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim Administration / Critics

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-01-13

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: Throughout 2025 and into 2026, Malaysia under Anwar Ibrahim maintained one of the most vocal stances globally on Palestinian rights — cutting diplomatic ties with Israel, boycotting Israeli-linked companies, and lecturing world leaders about “moral hypocrisy.” However, critics pointed out a glaring double standard: Malaysia’s silence on China’s persecution of Uyghur Muslims in Xinjiang, Iran’s brutal crackdown on internal dissent, the Yemen humanitarian crisis, and Myanmar’s genocide against Rohingya Muslims. Reddit discussions among Malaysians accused the government of “selective activism.” The Hindu reported Malaysia “slams double standards on Gaza atrocities” — while practising its own double standards on other Muslim-majority crises.

item.title: Anwar Lectures World on Gaza Hypocrisy — While Practising Hypocrisy on Every Reform Promise at Home
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim / NST Report Jan 2026

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-01-10

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: In January 2026, Anwar Ibrahim made international headlines lecturing global leaders about “moral hypocrisy” on Gaza, saying “global leadership is failing Gaza amid moral hypocrisy.” He has positioned himself as a leading voice for Palestinian rights on the world stage, attending summits, issuing condemnations, and cutting diplomatic ties with Israel. CSIS published an analysis titled “Death of Reformasi” examining how Anwar’s international posturing contrasts with his domestic record.

item.title: Will 2026 Be the Year PH Fulfils Its Reform Promises? — FMT Asks, Again
item.description:
Said by: FMT editorial

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-01-10

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: On 10 January 2026, FMT published an opinion piece titled “Will 2026 be the year PH fulfils its reform promises?” — the same question asked in January 2024 and January 2025. On 5 January, Anwar gave a New Year address promising that reform pledges would “materialise this year,” announcing the AG-PP separation bill would be “the first bill to be tabled.” The bill was indeed tabled — then immediately referred to a parliamentary committee for three more months of “review.” This promise has been pending since GE14 in 2018, now eight years unfulfilled. The reform scorecard after three full years: AG-PP separation sent to committee, UUCA “no plans” to abolish, Sedition Act used 90+ times instead of repealed, ICCPR unratified, toll abolition replaced by RM591M freezes, PM term limit bill failed by 2 votes, IPCMC not established, Freedom of Information Act not tabled, local government elections not restored.

item.title: Zahid Hamidi's 47 Corruption Charges NFA'd — 'Insufficient Evidence' After 7 Years
item.description:
Said by: Attorney-General’s Chambers

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-01-08

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: On 8 January 2026, the Attorney-General’s Chambers (AGC) ended all 47 criminal charges against Deputy Prime Minister Ahmad Zahid Hamidi, citing “insufficient evidence.” The charges — filed in 2018 after UMNO lost power — involved RM31 million from Yayasan Akalbudi, a charitable foundation Zahid controlled. The charges included criminal breach of trust, corruption, and money laundering. Reuters reported the AGC issued a discharge not amounting to an acquittal (DNAA) for some charges and a full acquittal for others. The Malaysian Bar objected. CSIS published an analysis titled “Death of Reformasi.” The Diplomat called it the dropping of charges against “a key Anwar ally.”

item.title: Anwar's Signature Move: Whataboutism — 'But BN Did It Too' Is Not a Reform Agenda
item.description:
Said by: Parliamentary Hansard / Media Analysis

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-01-01

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: A clear rhetorical pattern has emerged in Anwar Ibrahim’s parliamentary and public responses to criticism: deflect to what previous governments or the opposition did. When challenged on support letters for government tenders, he pointed to Kelantan. When challenged on lobbying, he brought up PKR’s past. When challenged on reform failures, he invokes the “mess we inherited.” This is systematic whataboutism deployed as governance strategy. The Star reported Anwar “calls out opposition’s hypocrisy over support letters” — turning a question about his own government’s conduct into an attack on the opposition. CSIS, in its “Death of Reformasi” analysis, noted how Anwar consistently deflects accountability by reframing criticism as opposition bad faith.

item.title: Contract Doctors: 33,000 STILL Waiting for Permanent Posts — Promise Made in 2020, Still Broken in 2026
item.description:
Said by: Malaysian Medical Association / Contract Doctors

Category: Health

Date: 2026-01-01

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: As of January 2026, approximately 33,000 contract doctors in Malaysia’s public healthcare system continue to work on temporary contracts without job security, pension benefits, or career advancement pathways available to permanent medical officers. The contract system was introduced as a “temporary” measure to absorb a surge of medical graduates. Six years later, it is still “temporary.” Contract doctors do the same work as permanent doctors — they see the same patients, work the same 84-hour weeks, handle the same emergencies — but without permanent employment status. CNA reported on the “doctor shortage, low pay, 84-hour working week” crisis. P4H Network described Malaysia’s healthcare system as a “ticking bomb.”

item.title: No GE16 Accountability Framework — PH Has No System for Voters to Track Manifesto Progress
item.description:
Said by: Governance Reform Advocates

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-01-01

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: Unlike some democracies where government manifesto progress is publicly tracked, PH has established no formal system for voters to monitor delivery against the approximately 60 promises made in GE15. There is no public dashboard, no annual manifesto progress report, and no structured mechanism for civil society to hold the government accountable to its specific commitments. Malaysiakini created an independent manifesto tracker because the government wouldn’t. The Straits Times tracked PH 1.0’s progress and found most promises “still had some way to go” after just one year.

item.title: Social Media Platforms Forcibly 'Deemed Licensed' — MCMC Tightens Grip on Internet
item.description:
Said by: Fahmi Fadzil, Communications Minister

Category: Social

Date: 2026-01-01

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: From 1 January 2026, social media and instant messaging platforms with 8 million or more Malaysian users were automatically “deemed licensed” under MCMC’s regulatory framework — whether they applied for a licence or not. This means platforms like Meta (Facebook, Instagram, WhatsApp), TikTok, Telegram, and X are now subject to Malaysian government licensing requirements, content takedown obligations, and penalties for non-compliance. Meta publicly warned the scheme “lacks clarity and threatens innovation.” Soyacincau reported that MCMC’s approach effectively bypassed the normal licensing application process by unilaterally deeming platforms as licensed. Reuters reported Meta’s concerns about the regulatory framework’s vagueness.

item.title: TikTok Generation Lost — Malaysian Youth Screen Time Among Highest in the World While Government Plans to Ban Under-16s
item.description:
Said by: MCMC / Online Safety Act

Category: Social

Date: 2026-01-01

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: Malaysian youth have among the highest screen times and social media usage rates in the world. The government’s response was the Online Safety Act, which bans social media use for under-16s from 2026. The law places enforcement responsibility on platforms, with penalties up to RM10 million for non-compliance. However, implementation details remain vague — no age verification infrastructure exists, no technical framework has been announced, and enforcement mechanisms are untested. Freedom House’s 2024 report flagged Malaysia’s declining internet freedom trajectory under PH.

item.title: UMNO's Stranglehold on PH — Zahid Walks Free While Dictating Coalition Policy
item.description:
Said by: Political Analysts / Opposition

Category: Politics

Date: 2026-01-01

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: The single most corrosive dynamic in Malaysian politics today is UMNO’s outsized leverage over PH within the unity government coalition. DPM Ahmad Zahid Hamidi — who had 47 corruption charges dropped in an NFA decision that shocked the legal community — now effectively holds veto power over government policy. UMNO’s support is essential for PH’s parliamentary majority, and UMNO knows it. The result: Zahid walks free, Najib gets sentence reductions and house arrest bids, the AG-PP separation is stalled, DAP is sidelined in the cabinet reshuffle, and the anti-corruption agenda is functionally dead. UCA News described the unity coalition as having a “fractured mandate.” CNA reported Anwar faces a “graft crisis” with “PKR party fractures” and “PH coalition jitters.”

item.title: Wages Haven't Kept Up with Costs — Real Purchasing Power Has DECLINED Under PH
item.description:
Said by: DOSM / BNM / Consumer Groups

Category: Economy

Date: 2026-01-01

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: While nominal wages have increased slightly under PH — minimum wage raised to RM1,500, modest salary increments across sectors — the cost of living has risen faster. Diesel is up 56%. SST was expanded to cover more essentials. Food prices are the highest in Southeast Asia at $1,940 per person per year according to USDA data. Electricity costs are up. Property prices are up. The result: real purchasing power — what your ringgit actually buys — has declined for most Malaysians. A 2025 consumer survey found 77% of Malaysians say costs have risen significantly, while official inflation claims just 1.3%.

item.title: Public Transport 'Functional But Fragile' — One Disruption and Everyone Drives
item.description:
Said by: Khazanah Research Institute (KRI)

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-12-31
