📌 WHAT HAPPENED: A Khazanah Research Institute (KRI) study published in late December 2025 found that Greater KL’s public transport system is “functional but fragile” — minor disruptions cause commuters to abandon it entirely for private vehicles. KRI concluded that rail was “never meant to fix Malaysia’s traffic problem.” The study found that fuel remains artificially cheap, parking is abundant and affordable, walking environments are fragmented and hostile, and last-mile connectivity is inadequate. FMT’s analysis argued it is “an unfinished system, not infrastructure, that fails Malaysian commuters.” Yet the government continues announcing mega rail projects — MRT3, LRT3, ECRL — as if building more tracks alone will solve mobility.

item.title: Najib House Arrest Bid Rejected — Court Rules Royal Addendum Invalid
item.description:
Said by: High Court Judge Alice Loke / Najib Razak

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-12-22

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: On 22 December 2025, the KL High Court ruled that the royal addendum allowing convicted former PM Najib Razak to serve his remaining 1MDB sentence under house arrest was invalid. Judge Alice Loke said the King’s discretion must be exercised “for the public good” and found the addendum lacked proper legal basis. Al Jazeera and Reuters both reported the ruling. The Selangor Sultan had earlier warned that pardons should not be “arbitrary for certain parties.” Anwar’s government had already halved Najib’s sentence via the Pardons Board in February 2024 — the house arrest bid was the next step in an escalating pattern of leniency for a convicted kleptocrat whose party Anwar now depends on.

item.title: Toll Abolition Promise Broken — Govt Pays RM591M to FREEZE Hikes Instead
item.description:
Said by: Fahmi Fadzil, Communications Minister

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-12-18

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: PH promised to abolish highway tolls in both GE14 and GE15. Instead, in December 2025, the government announced it would spend RM591 million just to prevent toll increases on 10 highways in 2026 — paying concessionaires with public money to not raise prices. The Works Minister admitted that fully abolishing tolls would cost “billions.” Gerakan mocked the broken promise. FMT asked whether Anwar would “repeat PH’s pledge to abolish tolls” ahead of GE16. Malaysian drivers continue paying tolls AND their taxes now subsidise the freeze. Festive toll-free periods were also cancelled.

item.title: Anwar Says 'Long Way to Go' on Elections in December 2025 — Now Preparing Snap GE16 for 2026
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-12-17

📌 WHAT HAPPENED

On December 17, 2025, Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim told editors and senior journalists at a briefing in Seri Perdana that he had ruled out any possibility of snap general elections in 2026. “The election still has a long way to go. I have not thought about it,” he said. “I am focusing on how we continue to implement reforms and alleviate the problems people face regarding the higher cost of living.”

item.title: Hannah Yeoh Appointment Sparks Race-Based Backlash — PAS and UMNO Both Object
item.description:
Said by: Takiyuddin Hassan, PAS Secretary-General

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-12-17

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: In December 2025, Anwar appointed Hannah Yeoh as Federal Territories Minister in his cabinet reshuffle. PAS Secretary-General Takiyuddin Hassan immediately objected — not to Yeoh’s qualifications, but to her race. He argued the appointment would “strengthen DAP’s influence” in urban areas and disadvantage Malays. Rightist Malay groups warned that DAP was now “controlling Malay landholding” through the Federal Territories portfolio, which oversees KL, Putrajaya, and Labuan. Even UMNO leaders quietly raised concerns. Anwar hit back: “To reject a person because of her colour and race, this is atrocious in this day and age.” Social media reactions were polarised.

item.title: Anwar's Cabinet Reshuffle Sparks Race Controversy — Hannah Yeoh as FT Minister, Adam Adli as Religious Affairs Deputy
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-12-16

📌 WHAT HAPPENED

On December 16, 2025, Anwar Ibrahim announced his second cabinet reshuffle, involving 28 ministerial and deputy ministerial changes. Among the most controversial appointments:

Hannah Yeoh was named Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Federal Territories) — the first non-Malay to hold the portfolio. PAS and right-wing Malay groups immediately attacked the appointment, claiming it would strengthen “DAP dominance” in KL and threaten Malay interests. Anwar called the criticism “atrocious” and said race should not determine ministerial capability.

item.title: Zafrul Appointed MIDA Chairman After Leaving MITI — The Revolving Door Never Stops Spinning
item.description:
Said by: Tengku Zafrul Abdul Aziz, MIDA Chairman

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-12-16

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: When the December 2025 cabinet reshuffle removed Tengku Zafrul from the MITI portfolio, he wasn’t sent to the backbench. Instead, Anwar appointed him as Chairman of MIDA (Malaysian Investment Development Authority) — the very agency that falls under MITI’s purview — and as Senior Political Advisor to the PM. The man who oversaw MIDA as minister now chairs it as a political appointee.

item.title: Civil Service is 90% Bumiputera — Malays Hold 80%+ of Top Posts
item.description:
Said by: Eurasia Review Analysis

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-12-15

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: According to Eurasia Review analysis published in December 2025, Malaysia’s civil service of 1.3 to 1.6 million employees is approximately 90% Bumiputera. Malays hold 77.5% of all posts and over 80% of top management positions. Chinese Malaysians hold just 5.7% and Indian Malaysians 3.8% — in a country where Chinese make up 23% and Indians 7% of the population. The Straits Times reported the “Bumiputera-first policy debate” has taken on a “heady mix of health, education and politics.” When a UiTM professor suggested opening Bumiputera-only programmes to other races to address specialist shortages, the backlash was immediate and the idea was shut down.

item.title: LRT3 Delayed AGAIN — Software Glitches, Failed Test Runs, RM475M Penalties Looming
item.description:
Said by: Anthony Loke, Transport Minister

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-12-15

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: The LRT3 line from Bandar Utama to Johan Setia — a multi-billion ringgit project intended to transform connectivity in the Klang Valley — has been postponed repeatedly due to signalling software failures and the inability to complete fault-free test runs. Transport Minister Anthony Loke warned contractors may face penalties exceeding RM475 million for the delays. The opening date remains unconfirmed. The Edge and Lowyat both reported the latest round of testing setbacks as the system failed to achieve the required reliability benchmarks.

item.title: Race-Based Economic Policies NEP Still Untouchable — 'Unanimous' Critics Ignored
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim Administration / Think Tanks

Category: Social

Date: 2025-12-15

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: Eurasia Review published a comprehensive analysis in December 2025 finding “almost unanimous opinion” among analysts that Malaysia’s race-based economic policies — rooted in the New Economic Policy (NEP) of 1971 — have failed their “overarching goal of national unity.” Yet the analysis concluded the government is “highly unlikely to abandon” them. The NEP and its successors (NDP, NVP, and now Anwar’s various economic frameworks) maintain Bumiputera preferences in government contracts, education, housing, and corporate equity. Despite 50+ years of these policies, Malaysia remains deeply divided along ethnic lines, brain drain continues at alarming rates, and economic inequality persists within the Bumiputera community itself.

item.title: PM's Own Aide Shamsul Iskandar Charged with Bribery — Anti-Corruption Crusade Eats Itself
item.description:
Said by: Reuters / Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-12-04

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: On 4 December 2025, Anwar Ibrahim’s senior political secretary Shamsul Iskandar Mohd Akin was charged with receiving bribes totalling RM176,829 from a local businessman. The charges allege Shamsul used his privileged position in the Prime Minister’s office to assist the businessman in obtaining mineral mining licences in Sabah, among other favours. Shamsul resigned just days before being charged, pleaded not guilty, and was granted bail. He called the charges an “attack” against him. Reuters reported the charges “significantly increased scrutiny on the premier’s pledge to crack down on corruption.” CNA reported Anwar now faces a “graft crisis” alongside “PKR party fractures” and “PH coalition jitters.”

item.title: Sabah Timber Corruption — Billions in Illegal Logging While Shamsul Iskandar Got Mining Licences from PMX's Office
item.description:
Said by: Environmental Groups / MACC Investigations

Category: Other

Date: 2025-12-04

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: Sabah, home to some of Malaysia’s last remaining primary rainforests, continues to face massive illegal and unsustainable logging driven by corruption in licence allocation. The crisis was brought into sharp focus when Anwar’s own senior political secretary, Shamsul Iskandar, was charged on 4 December 2025 with receiving bribes totalling RM176,829 from a local businessman for facilitating mineral mining licences in Sabah. Reuters reported the charges exposed corruption “emanating directly from the PM’s office.” The Shamsul case revealed how natural resource extraction in East Malaysia is systematically corrupted by political patronage — with the corruption pipeline running all the way to the Prime Minister’s doorstep.

item.title: 1MDB Debt Still RM50 Billion — Malaysia's Largest Fiscal Burden Under Anwar's Watch
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-12-03

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: As of December 2025, Malaysia’s 1MDB debt still stands at approximately RM50 billion — the largest single fiscal burden in the country’s history. RM42 billion has already been paid in principal and interest. RM29 billion has been recovered through enforcement actions. Approximately RM13 billion remains to be settled. Under Anwar’s watch, Najib Razak — the PM who presided over 1MDB — had his jail sentence halved by the Pardons Board and was subsequently granted house arrest. The rakyat continues to pay the debt while the man responsible serves a comfortable sentence.

item.title: Anwar Defends Support Letters in Parliament — Then Attacks Opposition for Doing the EXACT Same Thing
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim in Parliament / The Star

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-12-02

📌 What Happened

When confronted in parliament about his defence of ‘recommendation letters’ (surat sokongan) for government tenders, Anwar pivoted to attacking the opposition by revealing that Kelantan and Terengganu state officials had also issued similar letters. He said: ‘I must call out the hypocrisy — there was not a single word on the letters by the Kelantan and Terengganu exco asking for approval.’

item.title: Debt-to-GDP Still at 64.6% — Anwar Says Wait Until 2028 for Relief
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-12-02

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: Malaysia’s debt-to-GDP ratio stood at 64.6% in 2024 — above the government’s own 60% target ceiling set under the Public Finance and Fiscal Responsibility Act. In December 2025, Anwar told Bloomberg that debt pressure would “ease from end-2028” — six years into his tenure before fiscal health even begins to improve. New borrowings were reduced to RM75 billion from RM100 billion but remain massive. The 1MDB legacy alone still accounts for RM50 billion in liabilities. CNA reported on Malaysia’s “increased spending” alongside the “low debt ratio” aspiration that remains unfulfilled.

item.title: Anwar's Three-Year Report Card: Steady Economics, Broken Promises, Collapsing Reform Credibility
item.description:
Said by: Multiple analysts and media

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-12-01

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: As Anwar Ibrahim marked three years as Prime Minister in late 2025, multiple analysts and media outlets delivered a consistent verdict: steady economics, shattered reform credibility. GDP growth exceeded 5%, foreign investment hit record highs, and unemployment reached decade lows. But on reform — the actual reason PH was elected — the record is dismal: the Sedition Act was expanded with 90 arrests, UUCA remains intact, ICCPR is unratified, AG-PP separation was sent to committee after three years of delay, Zahid’s 47 corruption charges were dropped, MACC is under fire for politicisation, a journalist was arrested for asking a question, and Nurul Izzah was installed as PKR No. 2.

item.title: Highway Concessions: Rakyat Pays Tolls to Make Cronies Rich — Toll Abolition Promise Broken AGAIN
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim Administration / Transport Ministry

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-12-01

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: The PH government’s core manifesto promise to abolish or significantly reduce highway tolls has been systematically broken. Instead of toll abolition, the government spent RM591 million in 2025 to merely freeze toll hikes — paying concessionaires with public money to not raise prices they shouldn’t be charging in the first place. Festive toll-free periods were cancelled. The fundamental structure of highway concessions — where private companies collect guaranteed revenue from a captive public while the government underwrites their profits — remains completely unchanged. Abolishing tolls was a core PH campaign promise in both GE14 (2018) and GE15 (2022). Three years into government, the promise has been quietly shelved with no accountability.

item.title: National Debt Servicing Eats 16% of Revenue — Every Sixth Ringgit Goes to Paying Interest
item.description:
Said by: Ministry of Finance / Bloomberg

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-12-01

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: Malaysia’s debt servicing costs consume approximately 16% of federal government revenue — meaning roughly every sixth ringgit collected in taxes goes straight to paying interest on accumulated debt rather than funding schools, hospitals, or infrastructure. With the debt-to-GDP ratio at 64.6% — above the government’s own 60% target — and 1MDB legacy debt still totalling RM50 billion on the books, fiscal space remains severely constrained. Anwar told Bloomberg that debt pressure would only “ease from end-2028.” The Star reported that 1MDB debt remains “Malaysia’s largest fiscal burden.”

item.title: Penang's Planning Failures Exposed — Traffic, Floods, Vanishing Green Spaces After 16 Years of DAP
item.description:
Said by: Chow Kon Yeow / Penang Forum / Aliran

Category: Social

Date: 2025-12-01

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: In December 2025, Aliran published a comprehensive report documenting why ‘Penang’s development planning is failing its people.’ Residents demanded transparency as traffic congestion, floods, and vanishing green spaces exposed ‘systematic failures in planning’ under the DAP-led state government. The report detailed how development approvals continued without adequate infrastructure, environmental assessments were inadequate, and community input was sidelined. Penang Forum — a coalition of civil society groups — has been documenting these failures for years.

item.title: Zafrul: The Man Who Served Every PM — From Muhyiddin's Finance Minister to Anwar's Trade Minister to 'Senior Advisor'
item.description:
Said by: Tengku Zafrul Abdul Aziz

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-12-01

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: Tengku Zafrul Abdul Aziz has achieved what no other Malaysian politician has: serving in senior ministerial roles under three consecutive PMs from three different coalitions. He was Finance Minister under Muhyiddin (PN) and Ismail Sabri (BN-PN), then switched to PKR and became MITI Minister under Anwar (PH). After being reshuffled out in December 2025, he was immediately appointed Senior Political Advisor to the PM and MIDA Chairman. SCMP profiled him as ‘hot property’ — the man every PM wants.

item.title: Felda-Terengganu Land War — 15,000 Hectares Exploited for 40 Years Without Paying the State a Sen
item.description:
Said by: Terengganu State Government / Felda

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-11-30

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: A decades-long land dispute between the Federal Land Development Authority (Felda) and the Terengganu state government exploded in late November 2025 when the state issued trespass notices covering 10 plantations totalling approximately 15,000 hectares. Terengganu accused Felda of developing state land for over 40 years without paying premiums, taxes, or profit shares — essentially extracting billions in palm oil revenue from state land for free. FGV Holdings was also served notices. DPM Zahid eventually stepped in for negotiations.

item.title: PH Wins 1 Out of 20 Seats in Sabah — Anwar Says Defeat Won't Stop Delivery
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-11-30

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: On 30 November 2025, Pakatan Harapan was demolished in the Sabah state election. The coalition won just 1 out of 20 seats it contested — down from 7 previously. That is a 95% loss rate. Sabah-based parties dominated, driven by anti-federal sentiment and demands for greater state autonomy — particularly after a High Court ruling supporting Sabah’s claim to 40% of federal revenue. Political analyst Lee Kuok Tiung said plainly: “The Sabah election outcome shows Anwar’s coalition lost badly.” Anwar’s response: the defeat “won’t stop delivery” of pledges to Sabah.

item.title: Amirudin Rolls Out Flood Recovery Plan — After Selangor's Worst Floods Under His Watch
item.description:
Said by: Amirudin Shari, Selangor Menteri Besar

Category: Social

Date: 2025-11-29

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: On 29 November 2025, Amirudin rolled out a ‘major mobilisation’ for post-flood recovery as evacuee numbers surged across Selangor. Water from northern Selangor flooded into Kuala Langat and Sepang. JPS installed four pumps in Kuala Selangor with six more planned. A police officer went missing after a car skidded into a storm drain near KLIA during the floods. The Met Department withdrew severe rain warnings, but damage was extensive.

item.title: Perlis Floods 2025 — 34,000 Evacuated, New Mother Forced to Flee Weeks After Birth
item.description:
Said by: Government / NADMA

Category: Other

Date: 2025-11-25

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: In late November 2025, severe flooding across northern Malaysia — particularly in Perlis, Kedah, and Kelantan — displaced over 34,000 people. Reuters described scenes where floodwaters turned entire communities into what looked “like an ocean.” Among those evacuated was a new mother forced to flee just weeks after giving birth. A police officer went missing after a vehicle skidded into a storm drain near KLIA. The government announced a Climate Resilience Plan in response, and NADMA coordinated relief operations across affected states.

item.title: Social Media Ban for Under-16s — Enforcement Plan: TBD
item.description:
Said by: Fahmi Fadzil, Communications Minister

Category: Social

Date: 2025-11-24

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: Communications Minister Fahmi Fadzil announced in November 2025 that Malaysia would ban social media access for children under 16, following Australia’s lead. The ban was set for implementation in 2026. However, the NYT noted the announcement was “light on details” — no age verification infrastructure exists, no enforcement mechanism was outlined, and no timeline for implementation was provided. The government framed it as protecting children from online harms, but offered no technical plan for making it work.

item.title: Penang PTMP Procurement: No Open Tenders, Just 'Request for Proposals' for Billions in Contracts
item.description:
Said by: Chow Kon Yeow / DAP Penang / Civil Society Critics

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-11-21

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: Multiple reports, including from FMT and the academic community, documented that DAP’s Penang Transport Master Plan (PTMP) relied on ‘request-for-proposal’ (RFP) procurement mechanisms rather than open competitive tenders for billions of ringgit in infrastructure contracts. Civil society groups criticised this as opaque and developer-friendly. The PTMP includes the undersea tunnel (now under corruption trial), the Mutiara LRT, and various road projects — all procured without the standard open tender process that PH demands from the federal government.

item.title: Perak River Turns BLUE from Rare Earth Mining — Operations Suspended
item.description:
Said by: Johari Abdul Ghani, Environment Minister

Category: Other

Date: 2025-11-20

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: In November 2025, a stretch of the Perak River turned bright blue due to chemical discharges from a rare earth mining site. Environment Minister Johari Abdul Ghani confirmed the contamination and ordered operations suspended pending investigation. The incident occurred as the Malaysian government was aggressively pushing rare earth expansion — signing deals with the US and positioning Malaysia as a rival to China’s dominance in critical minerals. The blue river became a viral symbol of the environmental cost of the rare earth push.

item.title: Data Centre Boom Drains Water and Power — Johor Told to Wait Until 2027 for Water Supply
item.description:
Said by: Johor state government and Anwar Ibrahim

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-11-18

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: Malaysia’s data centre gold rush hit a wall in late 2025 when Johor — ground zero for the country’s silicon valley ambitions — told investors to postpone water-cooled projects for at least 18 months because the state couldn’t supply enough water. By February 2026, Anwar froze all non-AI data centre approvals entirely. Between 2023 and 2025, the government had approved billions in data centre projects from Microsoft, Google, AWS, Oracle, ByteDance, and GDS Holdings — without ensuring the infrastructure could support them. Malaysia’s national non-revenue water rate is 37.1%, meaning over a third of treated water is lost to leaking pipes before reaching consumers.

item.title: PM's Political Secretary Caught Issuing Support Letters for Hospital Contractors — Anwar Says a Reprimand Is Enough
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-11-18

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: On 18 November 2025, Anwar confirmed in Parliament that he had reprimanded his senior political secretary, Shamsul Iskandar Mohd Akin, over support letters listing six contractors for a hospital project in Johor. Anwar described the letters as “vague.” On 25 November, Shamsul resigned after mounting media pressure and calls from Rafizi Ramli for a MACC investigation. A Sabah businessman then claimed he provided RM152,000 to Shamsul — money allegedly spent on home furniture, premium cigars, and tailored suits. On 4 December 2025, MACC charged Shamsul with corruption over RM176,829 in gratification related to the hospital project.

item.title: Selangor MB's Aide Accused of Unexplained Wealth — RM3M Bungalow, Luxury Trips, and Too Many GLC Posts
item.description:
Said by: Amirudin Shari, Selangor Menteri Besar

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-11-15

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: In November 2025, a newly created WhatsApp group named ‘MB Selangor’ — comprising state assembly members and leaders — triggered controversy by accusing a PKR leader close to Selangor Menteri Besar Amirudin Shari of unexplained wealth. The administrator questioned how the leader could afford a RM3 million bungalow, luxury overseas trips, and high-end hotel stays while holding numerous positions in state-linked agencies. The group asked: ‘Why is one person appointed to so many positions, as if there are no other qualified people in Selangor?’

item.title: Penang Undersea Tunnel Project 'Still Active' — 14 Years, Zero Construction, RM6.3B Price Tag
item.description:
Said by: Chow Kon Yeow, Penang Chief Minister

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-11-14

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: On 14 November 2025, Chow Kon Yeow told media that the Penang Undersea Tunnel Project is ’not an abandoned project but is still active and in the technical review and design completion phase.’ The RM6.3 billion project was proposed in 2011 under the Penang Transport Master Plan (PTMP). Fourteen years later, not a single metre of tunnel has been constructed. Meanwhile, the corruption trial of former CM Lim Guan Eng over the project continues, with MACC witnesses describing it as ‘akin to a land scam.’

item.title: US Declares 'Victory Against China' After Securing Unlimited Minerals Pledge From Anwar
item.description:
Said by: MalaysiaNow report

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-11-03

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: On 3 November 2025, MalaysiaNow reported that the United States had “declared victory against China” after securing a pledge from Anwar Ibrahim to not restrict exports of critical minerals and rare earth elements to the US. Under the US-Malaysia ART, Malaysia committed to “refrain from banning, or imposing quotas on, exports to the US of critical minerals or rare earth elements” — a binding trade commitment, not a goodwill gesture. Malaysia also agreed to partner with American firms “to create certainty for businesses to increase production capacity,” essentially guaranteeing US access to Malaysian mineral resources in perpetuity.

item.title: 'Stop Lecturing Us on Sovereign Rights' — US Envoy Told Off Amid Anger Over Trade Deal
item.description:
Said by: Malaysian public and politicians

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-11-02

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: Days after the US-Malaysia trade deal was signed, public anger boiled over. The US envoy was publicly told to “stop lecturing us on sovereign rights” amid growing fury over what critics called Anwar’s “surrender” to Washington. MalaysiaNow reported the confrontation. The deal required Malaysia to align with US export controls, sanctions, and trade enforcement — provisions widely seen as infringing on sovereignty. Adding insult to injury, details of the agreement were published on the White House website before being shared with the Malaysian Parliament, fueling perceptions that Malaysia was an afterthought in its own trade negotiations.

item.title: Chow Admits Penang's 'Rapid Development' Not Felt by Some Communities — After 16 Years of DAP Rule
item.description:
Said by: Chow Kon Yeow, Penang Chief Minister

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-11-02

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: On 2 November 2025, Penang CM Chow Kon Yeow admitted that the state’s ‘rapid development’ was ’not felt by some communities.’ He urged DAP leaders to ‘stay close to the people and take care of the grassroots’ and said the party must focus on solving everyday problems like flooding, traffic congestion, water supply, and housing repairs. He acknowledged that improving ground-level performance would reinforce public confidence in DAP.

item.title: Lowy Institute: 'Anwar's International Success Sours at Home' — US Trade Deal Backlash
item.description:
Said by: Lowy Institute Analysis

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-11-01

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: In November 2025, the Lowy Institute published an analysis of the US-Malaysia trade deal (ART), concluding that “Anwar’s international success sours at home.” The analysis identified ‘poison pill’ clauses that function as a “loyalty test” requiring Malaysia to align with US strategic interests — including export controls, sanctions enforcement, and unrestricted critical mineral exports. The Lowy Institute noted that the 19% tariff on Malaysian goods was maintained, not reduced to zero as Anwar’s public framing implied. The deal was characterised as a US strategic win dressed up as a bilateral partnership.

item.title: Parliament Reduced to Rubber Stamp — PM Whips Ensure Bills Pass Without Genuine Debate
item.description:
Said by: Opposition MPs / Parliamentary Reform Advocates

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-11-01

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: Despite PH’s promise to strengthen parliamentary democracy, the Dewan Rakyat continues to function as a rubber stamp rather than a genuine deliberative body. Government bills pass with minimal amendment, committee work is perfunctory, and the government whip ensures coalition MPs vote in bloc. The PM term limit bill — one of PH’s signature reform promises — failed in Parliament because PH couldn’t even muster enough of its own MPs to support it. Fahmi Fadzil blamed “opposition confusion” for the failure, when 8 of PH’s own MPs didn’t show up to vote.

item.title: Personal Bankruptcies Rise 5.7% — Self-Declared Bankruptcy Trend 'Growing Concern'
item.description:
Said by: Department of Insolvency Malaysia

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-11-01

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: In the first nine months of 2025, Malaysia recorded 4,875 personal bankruptcy cases — a 5.7% increase from the same period in 2024. The Insolvency Department Director-General flagged a “growing concern”: the rise in self-declared bankruptcies, where Malaysians are so financially distressed they voluntarily declare themselves bankrupt rather than waiting for creditors to force the process. The data indicates that financial distress is not concentrated among business owners — it is spreading to salaried workers, young professionals, and gig economy participants.

item.title: Rakyat Says 1.3% Inflation is a Lie — 'Don't Know Where Madanon Gets These Numbers'
item.description:
Said by: Public Sentiment / Social Media

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-11-01

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: Malaysia’s official inflation rate for October 2025 was reported at 1.3% by the Department of Statistics (DOSM). Malaysians responded with open mockery on social media, with comments like “don’t know where Madanon gets these numbers.” The disconnect between headline inflation and lived experience is stark: food-away-from-home inflation ran at 4.3-4.7%, far exceeding the headline rate. Rental costs, healthcare, and education expenses — categories that disproportionately affect middle and lower-income households — all rose faster than the official composite figure suggests.

item.title: Youth Unemployment Stuck at 12.28% — One in Eight Young Malaysians Can't Find Work
item.description:
Said by: DOSM / ILO / World Bank Data

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-11-01

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: As of late 2025, Malaysia’s youth unemployment rate (ages 15-24) remains stuck at 12.28% — roughly 297,500 unemployed young Malaysians. This is three to four times higher than the overall national unemployment rate of 3.2%. DOSM, the ILO, and World Bank data all confirm the structural nature of the problem: Malaysia produces graduates the economy doesn’t need, while industries that are hiring can’t find workers with the right skills. The “Madani economy” that Anwar celebrates apparently doesn’t include young people.

item.title: Zafrul: 'We Had No Choice But to Negotiate' — The Most Honest and Damning Defence of the US Trade Deal
item.description:
Said by: Tengku Zafrul Abdul Aziz, MITI Minister

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-10-29

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: Speaking in Parliament on 29 October 2025, Tengku Zafrul delivered what he intended as a defence of the ART but what critics heard as an admission of weakness: ‘We had no choice but to negotiate. Whether we like it or not, we need to defend our access to the US markets, even when under pressure.’ He called the deal the ‘best case scenario’ and accused detractors of ‘ignorance regarding the intense, behind-the-scenes negotiations.’

item.title: Selangor Water: 27% Lost to Leaks — Amirudin Touts Smart Meters While Pipes Burst
item.description:
Said by: Amirudin Shari, Selangor Menteri Besar

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-10-27

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: On 27 October 2025, Amirudin told Parliament that pilot smart water meter projects in Selangor had proven effective — citing Bukit Jelutong where non-revenue water (NRW) was recorded at 27%. He urged the federal government to adopt smart meters nationwide. Separately, the Selangor government announced it would absorb increased water costs for over one million users and accelerate pipe replacement at 300km per year, targeting a 20% water reserve margin by 2030.

item.title: Malaysia Committed to Never Banning Rare Earth Exports to the US — Under Zafrul's Trade Deal
item.description:
Said by: Tengku Zafrul Abdul Aziz, MITI Minister / USTR

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-10-26

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: Under the US-Malaysia Agreement on Reciprocal Trade signed by Tengku Zafrul on 26 October 2025, Malaysia committed to not imposing ‘quantitative restrictions’ (bans or quotas) on exports of critical or rare-earth minerals to the United States. The USTR fact sheet explicitly highlights this as a US victory. Meanwhile, Zafrul dismissed Mahathir’s claim that Malaysia agreed to supply unprocessed rare earth at low returns, saying existing policy mandates domestic processing before export.

item.title: RTM Misidentifies THREE ASEAN Leaders on Live TV — Calls Singapore's PM 'Lee Hsien Loong' at Summit Malaysia Hosted
item.description:
Said by: RTM / Communications Ministry under Fahmi Fadzil

Category: Politics

Date: 2025-10-26

📌 WHAT HAPPENED

On 26 October 2025, during the live broadcast of the opening ceremony of the 47th ASEAN Summit — which Malaysia was hosting — the national broadcaster Radio Televisyen Malaysia (RTM) misidentified three Southeast Asian leaders:

  • Singapore PM Lawrence Wong was called Lee Hsien Loong (his predecessor)
  • Thailand PM Anutin Charnvirakul was called Srettha Thavisin (his predecessor)
  • Indonesia President Prabowo Subianto was called Jokowi (his predecessor) RTM issued two separate apology statements. The broadcasting department said it “took the matter seriously” and that “appropriate action had been taken.”

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS

This was Malaysia’s moment on the world stage. The ASEAN Summit is the region’s premier diplomatic event, and Malaysia was the host. The entire region was watching. And Malaysia’s national broadcaster couldn’t get the names of three neighbouring heads of state right.

item.title: Tengku Zafrul Negotiated the US Trade Deal Critics Call 'Worst Agreement Since Independence' — Then Got Promoted
item.description:
Said by: Tengku Zafrul Abdul Aziz, former MITI Minister

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-10-26

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: On 26 October 2025, then-MITI Minister Tengku Zafrul signed the Agreement on Reciprocal Trade (ART) with the United States at the 47th ASEAN Summit in Kuala Lumpur. The deal committed Malaysia to providing preferential market access for US products across chemicals, machinery, vehicles, dairy, poultry, pork, rice, and fuel ethanol. Malaysia also committed to not imposing bans or quotas on the export of critical or rare-earth minerals to the US. In return, the US maintained a 19% reciprocal tariff on Malaysian goods — with only 60.4% of tariff lines zero-rated. Critics from across the political spectrum, including Mahathir and Rafizi Ramli, called it a one-sided surrender.

item.title: US Trade Deal Details Published on White House Website Before Malaysian Parliament Was Told
item.description:
Said by: Tengku Zafrul Abdul Aziz, MITI Minister / White House

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-10-26

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: When the US-Malaysia Agreement on Reciprocal Trade was signed on 26 October 2025, the USTR published a detailed fact sheet on the White House website outlining Malaysia’s commitments — including preferential market access for US goods, commitments on rare earth minerals, and tariff schedules. Malaysian MPs and the public learned the details of their own country’s trade agreement from an American government website. The deal’s terms were published in Washington before they were presented in Putrajaya.

item.title: US-Malaysia Trade Deal Called 'Worst Agreement Since Independence' and 'Act of Surrender'
item.description:
Said by: Tommy Thomas, Former Attorney General (and critics)

Category: Economy

Date: 2025-10-26

The Deal That Sold Malaysia’s Sovereignty

On 26 October 2025, Anwar Ibrahim and Donald Trump signed the US-Malaysia Agreement on Reciprocal Trade (ART) at the ASEAN Summit. Former Attorney-General Tommy Thomas called it the worst agreement Malaysia has entered since independence in 1957. He was not exaggerating.

The Facts

Fact: The deal maintains a 19% US tariff on Malaysian goods — down from 25%, but still punitive by any standard. Malaysia’s effective tariff on US goods before this deal was roughly 6%. The US got the better end by a factor of three.
